violations.text: Prep unit in kitchen was observed in poor repair. Unit was out of service prior to inspection. No foods can be placed in unit until repaired and can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/21/2025
The ceiling and walls in the prep room are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/16/2025

The kitchen hood and surrounding stove areas have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2025
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Owner states that additional staff will be enrolled in food safety course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. PIC to maintain refrigerator logs and hot holding logs. Logs to be available upon re-inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025

Bolos de papas and bolos de yuca were observed held at 123 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were reheated during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025

Various foods in the prep unit cooler were observed held at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Time/temperature abused foods were voluntarily disposed of. Foods that were recently placed in unit were placed in other refrigeration. Prep unit was unplugged and taken out of service during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025

Prep unit in kitchen was observed at 51 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was taken out of service during time of inspection. Unit was unplugged. Unit must be properly adjusted and/or replaced and can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 07/21/2025

Exterior evaporator enclosure was observed with an accumulation of leaves, litter and trash. Area to be maintained cleaned to prevent harborage conditions and proper mechanical functions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025
The ceiling and walls in the prep room are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/16/2025
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Owner states that additional staff will be enrolled in food safety course. New Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Temperature logs for refrigeration provided to establishment. Logs to be maintained and available for inspector review upon re-inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. State written agreement provided to establishment during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. State guidance provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025

Pork chops and chile rellenos were observed held at 114 and 128 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were reheated during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025

Various foods in the walk-in cooler were observed held at 46-51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Time/temperature abused foods were voluntarily disposed of. Technician arrived during inspection and serviced walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025
Various Foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025
Prep unit in kitchen was observed at 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was taken out of service to hold foods that require refrigeration for food safety. New Violation. Correct By: 07/21/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of food containers have an accumulation of sticky residue and old labels. New Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025

Exterior evaporator enclosure was observed with an accumulation of leaves, litter and trash. Area to be maintained cleaned to prevent harborage conditions and proper mechanical functions. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

The floors and walls the kitchen preparation area (especially under equipment and prep table) have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) was observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was actively being cleaned during time of inspection. Slicer cleaning and sanitizing aid provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024
The condensate from the deli display case is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
Coving by back door was observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024
Allergen advisory statement was lacking at hot food service counter. Sign was placed at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

Both handwashing sinks in food preparation area and back prep room were observed with miscellaneous items in basin. No soap and paper towels were observed at handsinks. Handsinks were cleared of miscellaneous items and soap and paper towels were placed at handsinks. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
Arepas con queso in the reach-in/walk-in cooler were observed with mold to sight. Food was placed in back stock area to return for credit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. 3 bay sink was set up during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

Salted Anchovies and Shrimps were observed held for sale at room temperature, label states to keep food at 38 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Food was placed in back stock area to return for credit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

Various prepared foods in walk-in cooler were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

Arepas, pickled vegetables, and yogurt drinks were observed for retail sale exceeding expiration dates and sell by dates. Food was placed in back stock area to return for credit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

The presence of small flies is not controlled as evidenced by live insects observed during time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024
Food and single service items were observed stored on the floor in the basement. Food and single service items must be at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/27/2024

The condensate from the deli display case is draining into a container. Condenser was observed leaking in walk-in cooler 2. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain or other appropriate method. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024
Coving by back door was observed in poor repair. Physical facilities must be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024

Kitchen hood vents were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/27/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was in process of being cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Guidance on how to clean slicer provided during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024

Fried Green Beans, Fried chicken and pastelitos were observed held at (122-131) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were reheated to a minimum of 165 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Units were also adjusted to higher temperatures. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024

Ham, bacon, salami, and raw pork were observed held at (43-49) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Ham was voluntarily disposed of. All other foods were placed into other refrigeration to blast chill. Product was observed stored in excess in deli case. Establishment must not store foods beyond load limit. Ambient of unit was 38 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2024
Handwashing signs are lacking at all handwashing sinks. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as milk) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) was observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

Various foods in deli display case were observed held at (46-51) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All TCS foods were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Equipment taken out of service to hold TCS/PHF foods. Potentially hazardous foods cannot be placed in this cooler until foods can hold an internal temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

Chicharron packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Food was removed from self-service sale during time of inspection. Packaged foods must be properly labelled when placed out for self-service sale. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024
Opening was observed under 3 bay sink. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024
